full_text:
Myelonets
3D CyCiF

The most important result from 3D!

We conclude that EMT-like transitions and TB in CRC1 is characterized not by the formation of isolated spheres of cells, as first described by Weinberg and colleagues in tissue culture,48 but instead by the formation of large fibrillar structures that appear to be small buds when viewed in cross-section at their distal tips.
Fibrils can invade into several different environments, including stroma and mucin, and we speculate that their formation is driven by a gradual (not abrupt) breakdown in cell adhesion associated with a graded EMT-like transition

3D on immune composition

However, we found that many of these domains were interconnected, forming larger 3D structures; for example, seven large networks, each spanning >12 sections and several millimeters laterally.

TLSs undergo maturation and are expected to differ from one another, but when we mapped marker expression clusters onto the physical organization of TLSNs, we found that some were relatively homogeneous, containing cells from one expression cluster, whereas others were heterogeneous. 

For example, TLSN-C, which was predominantly located in the subserosa, was >96% composed of expression cluster 7 and showed a marked predominance of CD45+ CD20+ B cells

Immune profiling of the invasive margin

(L) High-resolution 3D imaging of PDL1:PD1 interaction among tumor and myeloid cells. 

Top: maximum intensity projections. Bottom: 3D rendering, Imaris

Cell types involved in presenting PDL1 to PD1+ T cells

Although a small minority (1%–5%) of tumor cells expressed PDL1, the cells most likely to be PDL1+ were CD68+ (14%–51% positive) and CD11c+ myeloid cells (10%–88% positive); PDL1+ myeloid cells were also 6.5-fold more abundant on average than PDL1+ tumor cells (Figures 7F and S6E).

Second the most important result from the article!

Across the CRC cohort, we found that single positive CD68+ CD11c or CD68CD11c+ and double positive CD68+ CD11c+ cells were commonly PDL1+

Speaker notes:
We do not have the markers in our panels to subtype more precisely PDL1+ myeloid populations, but our interpretation is that they include variable proportions of macrophages, dendritic cells, and other mononuclear phagocytes.

PD1-PDL1 binding

Functionally, it is not the prevalence of PDL1+ cells that is relevant for T cell suppression but rather which cells are close enough for PDL1:PD1 binding

cells interacting with PD1+ cells were strongly enriched for CD45+ and depleted for CK+ (p < 0.001 pairwise t test, two-sided), showing that PD1+ T cells interact with PDL1+ immune cells more commonly than PDL1+ tumor cells.

Finally, high-resolution optical sectioning of 12-plex CyCIF provided direct evidence of PDL1+ on myeloid cells colocalizing with PD1+ T cells at the tumor margin, consistent with the formation of functional cell-cell interactions (Figure 7L). 

We conclude that immunosuppression of PD1+ T cells in our CRC cohort most commonly involves PDL1+ myeloid cells, not tumor cells

Main insights from the article

EM transition (EMT) occurs on the tumor margin (cells are still connected, not isolated, but form fibrils - result from 3D reconstruction, not visible in 2D)
Question that can be asked here - can we found also any spatially resolved structures of immune cells that will show association between immune cell type and cells undergoing EMT?

Immunosuppression in tumors is provided by interaction of PD1+ T cells with PDL1+ myeloid cells, all myeloid cells expressed PDL1.
Question - what type of myeloid cells do we see in myelonets (suppressive or not)? Can we characterise them in more details? Do they have something in common with EMT regions?

Speaker notes:
Maybe EMT regions are surrounded by myeloid cell types?

More detailed small questions

Myelonets - in which group of patients they are presented? 
responders vs. non-responders / before therapy vs. after therapy (paired samples are required) / mutated vs. wt)
Abundance of myeloid cells between groups
Which myeloid cell types they are consists of?
Include M1(CD68+, anti-tumor*) and M2 (CD163+, pro-tumor) markers MDSC cells -? 
Cell-cell interactions (?) 
Myeloid cells - tumor cells
Myeloid cells - Ki67+ tumor cells 
Myeloid cells - lymphocytes populations
Myeloid cells - PD1+ T cells
Myeloid PDL1+ cells - PD1+ T cells
3D reconstruction - do we see any specific region of tumor which interacts with myeloid cells? May be EMT regions?

* Better distinguish between M1 and M2

Transcription factors should also be studied
pSTAT1 for M1
CMAF for M2
https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0080908 (IHC)

Existing studies

https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-022-05680-3 the study was done on brain tumors, but a lot of attention was paid to the macrophages in TME and their association with prognosis.

Kaplan–Meier analysis based on MPO+ IBA1+ cell frequency as determined by IHF staining in 135 tumours from patients with glioblastoma (z-score). Cell frequencies were averaged when multiple samples corresponded to the same individual. Log-rank (Mantel–Cox) test

Studied also cell interactions and showed that they are not random and do not correlate with cell frequencies

2.https://aacrjournals.org/cancerimmunolres/article/9/6/665/666167/Chemotherapy-Induces-Tumor-Associated-Macrophages
Used unmatched samples for pre and post (??)
Showed that CD68+ macrophages “lakes” are associated with good prognosis following chemotherapy
Frequency of CD163+ (M2) TAMs decreased after chemotherapy (but samples were unmatched!)

Existing studies

Pre-selected samples

Table with patients selection: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1WFUiFqqeyxgsK4QTlxfdIgU121LQW01nqbrYx5sRp9o/edit?usp=sharing

Presentation with HEs
https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1-8yd7birOFw8cDBFPt_tInu2pwZmuSVsrvYnzPisWlE/edit?usp=sharing

❌

❌

Suggested panel

Put PD1 and PDL1 in first cycles?

Cell combinations I expect

CD68+ - macrophages
CD163+ - M2 macrophages
CD68+ CD163- - M1 macrophages (??)
CD45+ CD4+ - T helpers
CD45+ CD8+ - cytotoxic T cells
CD11c+ - dendritic cells (?)
PD1+ CD4/PD1+ CD8 T cells
PAX8, PanCK - cancer cells
Ki67+ and/or PCNA+ - proliferating cells
PAX8+/PanCK+ Ki67+/PCNA+ - proliferating tumor cells 
Vimentin+, aSMA+ - stromal cells
MDSC subsets (https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00262-015-1782-5)
CD33+ CD11b+ = all MDSC
CD14+ CD15- and any myeloid marker (CD11b or CD33) = monocytic MDSC
CD14- CD15+ and any myeloid marker (CD11b or CD33) = PMN MDSC (granulocytic)
HLA-DRlow CD14+ = MDSC4 (a subset which amount in peripheral blood is associated with response in many cancers)
PDL1+ myeloid cells (different subtypes)

CD11b - pan-myeloid marker
CD11c - dendritic cells

3D: distance between sections

"Due to whole slide structural changes that biologically occur in the 5 μm space between sections, it was not possible to adequately register whole slide images this accurately without using non-rigid transformations, which resulted in imaging artifacts that skewed analysis."
https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2022.12.09.519807v1.abstract

Average cell size of lymphoid cells is 8-10 μm
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK459471/#:~:text=From%20the%20morphological%20point%20of,mitochondria%2C%20ribosomes%2C%20and%20lyzosomes.

3D visualization

“For CRC1, 106 serial sections were cut from an 1.7 x 1.7 cm piece of FFPE tissue and 22 H&E and 25 CyCIF images were collected, skipping some sections to increase the total dimension along the Z-axis”


Imaris: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1cyKqe58luBxH4ikf0TSJvxh6Z2zW6Pd-dG1_I92nTWo/edit?usp=sharing

https://www.cell.com/cell/pdf/S0092-8674(22)01571-9.pdf

Why 3D ?

"At the tumor invasive margin, where tumor, normal, and immune cells compete, T cell suppression involves multiple cell types and 3D imaging shows that seemingly localized 2D features such as tertiary lymphoid structures are commonly interconnected and have graded molecular properties"
Our data show that 3D reconstruction provides additional insight into the large-scale connectivity of biological structures, but for relatively straightforward tasks such as cell-type enumeration, 2D WSI is often adequate
